evals.name: filter-cs-as-noise-trap
evals.prompt: Our Hindi-language chatbot is performing poorly. Logs show ~40% of user messages are Hinglish (Hindi-English mixed). Should we filter those out of training?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — Hinglish is the norm for many users, NOT noise; (2) filtering 40% of conversational data leaves model trained on register that doesn't match deployment; (3) recommend treating CS as first-class via LinCE / GLUECoS data; (4) per-paragraph LID + CS-aware tokenizer; (5) flag English-trained Hindi model fails at CS in characteristic ways.

evals.name: matrix-language-frame
evals.prompt: How should I think about Spanglish text — is it Spanish with English words or English with Spanish words?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) introduce Matrix Language Frame model (Myers-Scotton); (2) Spanglish typically has Spanish or English as matrix language depending on speaker context; (3) detection methods exist for matrix-vs-embedded distinction; (4) implications for tokenizer + corpus stratification; (5) cite Poplack 1980.

evals.name: arabic-dialect-cs
evals.prompt: Building an Arabic chatbot. Do I need to handle MSA + dialect Arabic separately?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) YES — MSA + dialects (Egyptian, Levantine, Maghrebi, Gulf) routinely alternate in real text; (2) MADAR + DART corpora handle this; (3) per-dialect LID + tokenizer-aware policy; (4) Arabic chatbot trained on MSA-only fails at colloquial deployments; (5) cross-reference linguistic-scope for Arabic macrolanguage disambiguation.
